title: caruelina intercedens
side_effects: Caruelina intercedens can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues. Ingesting the plant's extract can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged use may lead to kidney damage and liver toxicity. Additionally, improper use can cause interactions with medications, including blood thinners, and exacerbate underlying health conditions like high blood pressure and diabetes. In severe cases, overexposure to the plant's toxic compounds can lead to seizures, coma, and even death.
